title: Ralph Phase O: RoPE compute_kernel_config (LoFi speed lever)
insight: attn.rope is the 3rd hottest decode op (134us/call non-trace). ttnn.experimental.rotary_embedding_llama accepts an optional compute_kernel_config but attention.py never passes one. Default fidelity is HiFi2. RoPE is a trig multiply (cos/sin multiplies) — LoFi should suffice numerically because the input range is bounded. Test LoFi on RoPE to see if it saves cycles without PCC loss.

stories.title: Wire BITNET_ROPE_LOFI env + test LoFi on decode RoPE
stories.finding: Wired BITNET_ROPE_LOFI env flag and passed ttnn.experimental.rotary_embedding_llama a LoFi math_fidelity compute_kernel_config. Tested on p150a: decode_tps 72.93 t/s (vs 74.04 mean baseline, -1.11 — outside 0.5 noise band). Greedy output changed from baseline pattern, strongly suggesting PCC degraded too (not measured in detail since speed alone rules out a win).
acceptanceCriteria: Env flag + plumbing added and tested — DONE then REVERTED
Speed measured with flag=1 — DONE (72.93 t/s)
Speed delta >= +0.5 requirement — NOT MET (-1.11 regression)

Revert (fully removed, not left as opt-in) — DONE. The experiment shows LoFi on this RoPE kernel either picks slower code paths or pulls extra data format conversions; fused RoPE already hits its own floor.

stories.title: Append Phase O finding to optimization-ceiling doc + commit
stories.finding: Session 14 doc updated with Phase O falsification record. PRD closure committed.
acceptanceCriteria: PRD closure commit + push — DONE (see commit)
